channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: We've barely seen AMD's RDNA 4 GPUs in action yet, but a new rumor suggests we could be getting an Nvidia RTX 5090 competitor at last
item.description: Nvidia is, as usual, leading the GPU market, but if this new rumor is true, there could be some serious competition down the line.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to strengthen the security of Oracle applications without relying on OAU
item.description: Vendor support agreements have long been a sticking point for customers, and the Oracle Applications Unlimited (OAU) program is no different.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple contemplates SMB, enterprise as its next major revenue stream as it launches a new partner program
item.description: Apple targets SMBs and enterprises with revamped partner program, aimed at boosting Mac sales and expanding business footprint.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel
item.description: The intelligence community has increasingly prioritized OSINT in recent years. Now, House lawmakers are creating a new subcommittee dedicated to the discipline. The post OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google’s online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lauren Sanchez alludes to ‘turbulence’ in life after ‘obscene’ Trump inauguration display
item.description: The fiancee of billionaire Jeff Bezos alluded to a rough start of 2025, several weeks after Megyn Kelly called her a 'hooker' for wearing a lacy white bustier top beneath a jacket to Trump's swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI agent could help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help customers with one of the more difficult and confusing purchase decisions: buying a car.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco data center switches feature baked-in security for AI, networking duties
item.description: Cisco has unwrapped a new family of data center switches it says will help customers more securely support large workloads and facilitate AI development across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Major Tech Companies Unite to Boost Online Safety for Kids with ROOST Initiative
item.description: Leading tech companies, including OpenAI and Discord, have raised $27 million for the Robust Online Safety Tools (ROOST) initiative, aimed at creating open-source tools to enhance online safety for kids.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Your ‘new’ Seagate data center hard drive is likely a used one
item.description: A growing number of buyers have reported purchasing supposedly new Seagate data center-grade hard drives, only to discover that they had been previously used for thousands of hours.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups
item.description: GSA senior leaders are saying the Trump administration’s long-term goal is to merge GSA, OPM and OMB into a single agency. The post GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Oracle maintains hold on JavaScript trademark
item.description: Oracle, under external pressure to yield control over the JavaScript trademark, has instead filed a motion for dismissal of part of a petition to cancel the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
